content.problemStatement:
JJ has a binary string SSS of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N. He wants to partition it into MMM substrings such that:


1≤M≤2⋅N1 \le M \le 2 \cdot N1≤M≤2⋅N
Each SiS_iSi​ belongs to exactly one partition
None of the partitioned substrings is a palindrome
For example: One of the valid partitions of 100100111001001110010011 is 100‾ 10‾ 011‾\underline{100}\ \underline{10}\ \underline{011}100​ 10​ 011​.

Can you find any partition satisfying the above conditions? If no such partition exists, output −1-1−1.

A string is called palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. 100110011001 and 001000010000100 are palindromic strings.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — half the length of the binary string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains a binary string SSS of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N containing 000s and 111s only.


Output Format
For each test case,



In the first line, output: MMM (1≤M≤2⋅N)(1 \le M \le 2 \cdot N)(1≤M≤2⋅N) - the number of partitions
In the second line, output MMM integers: L1,L2,…,LML_1, L_2, \dots, L_ML1​,L2​,…,LM​ - where LiL_iLi​ denotes the length of the ithi^{th}ith partitioned substring. (Note that L1+L2+⋯+LM=2⋅NL_1 + L_2 + \dots + L_M = 2 \cdot NL1​+L2​+⋯+LM​=2⋅N and Li≥1L_i \ge 1Li​≥1 for all iii)
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Explained in the problem statement.
Test Case 2: It can be proven that no valid partition of 000000 exists.
Test Case 3: One of the valid partitions of 101101101101101101 is 10‾ 1101‾\underline{10}\ \underline{1101}10​ 1101​.
